claim: Creating separate agent conversations for each discrete coding task prevents context window overload and improves quality
actionable: Create new chat sessions in Cursor for each separate feature or task rather than appending to one long conversation
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers

claim: Having linters, formatters, and tests configured allows agents to self-correct their code without manual intervention
actionable: Set up TypeScript/linting, code formatting, and test suites so agents can automatically validate and fix their own outputs
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers

claim: Explicit file tagging in agent prompts ensures relevant context is included in the model's working memory
actionable: Explicitly tag relevant files in agent prompts to include them in context and ensure the model has necessary information
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers

claim: Custom slash commands in Cursor can encode best practices like security reviews, testing, and caching patterns
actionable: Build custom slash commands that guide agents through software engineering checklists specific to your project
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers

claim: Cursor agents can run headlessly via CLI in CI/CD pipelines for automated code reviews and bug fixes
actionable: Integrate Cursor CLI into GitHub Actions or CI systems to automate security audits, doc updates, and bug fixes
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers

claim: Cursor agents can be triggered from Slack to investigate and fix bugs directly from issue reports
actionable: Set up Cursor bot integration with Slack to allow on-demand bug investigation and PR creation from messages
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers

claim: Very high context window usage (80%+) causes quality degradation even with models that support 1M token contexts
actionable: Keep context usage below 80-90%; start new conversations rather than trying to use full context window capacity
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers

claim: When marginal coding cost approaches zero, developers build custom debugging interfaces and visualization tools they wouldn't have before
actionable: Consider building throwaway debugging tools and visualizations that were previously economically unfeasible
source_title: Cursor AI Agents Work Like 10 Developers
